body.title: [sprint] Inference router: compose the 4 stacks into a dimensional resolver (alongside route())
body.intention: I intend that resolve(RouteRequest) turns dimensions into a concrete (provider, model) by composing the 4 stacks, reusing the existing cost-optimizing selector, with the escalation walk gated by escalation_allowed.
body.description:
Compose the resolver (the pipeline of D-inference-router-stack-decomposition-2026-07-08), built ALONGSIDE the working route(). resolve(RouteRequest): dimensions -> policy rules narrow to a capability envelope -> candidate MODELS meeting it -> their CONNECTIONS filtered by provider availability + urgency/time eligibility -> the EXISTING cost-optimizing selector (cost_class, marginal dollars, priority) picks the cheapest capable connection. The within-domain escalation walk bumps difficulty up one rung per capability failure, ending in a system_alarm past the top rung; escalation_allowed=False pins the walk off.

**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/inference/rules_engine.py (a resolve() method beside route()); routing_buckets.py (REUSE the eligibility filters + cost-optimizing sort unchanged); tests/inference/test_resolver_compose.py. READ: connections.py, models_registry.py, sources.py.
**Design rules:** inference-tier-not-model; inference-cost-optimizing-router (reuse, don't reimplement the selector); docs-live-in-code; test-plan-or-why-not.
**Scope boundary:** add resolve() ALONGSIDE route(); do NOT modify or delete route()/_DEFAULT_RULES yet. Additive. Reuse the cost-optimizing selector verbatim — only its INPUT changes (connection candidates, not hardcoded triples).
**Test plan:** resolve(RouteRequest) returns a (provider, model) for given dimensions with NO hardcoded triple involved; escalation_allowed=False yields a deterministic single pick; escalation_allowed=True reproduces the difficulty-bump walk terminating in system_alarm when no capable connection exists. Red before resolve() exists -> green after.
**Rollback:** remove resolve() + test; route() still serves all live dispatch.
**Logging:** log the resolve crossing (dims -> chosen provider/model) at INFO; each escalation bump at INFO; the terminal system_alarm at WARNING.
**Depends on:** T-inference-connections-stack, T-inference-dimensions-contract, T-inference-rules-as-policy.
**Observability:** the resolve crossing log line shows the full dims->pick path; escalation bumps are individually logged.
